question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user cause sidecar records to be associated with RecordItems in the wrong order by exploiting the non-deterministic ordering of Flux.flatMap (which does not preserve order) at line 120, so that when multiple sidecar files are downloaded concurrently and their records share the same consensusTimestamp, the ArrayListMultimap at line 123 accumulates them in an arbitrary order, causing incorrect sidecar record ordering in the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getSidecarType()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a recoverable error to be logged but processing to continue with SIDECAR_TYPE_UNKNOWN_VALUE (0) for an unknown sidecar type, exploiting Utility.handleRecoverableError() at line 170-173 being non-fatal, so that when acceptedTypes is empty (all types accepted), the unknown-type record is included in the mirror node export with an incorrect type classification, corrupting the exported contract execution data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the entire sidecar download pipeline to be skipped for a RecordFile that has sidecars by crafting conditions where both `!downloaderProperties.isWriteFiles()` and `!sidecarProperties.isEnabled()` are true at line 111, exploiting the short-circuit OR logic, so that even though the record file has sidecar entries, none are downloaded or associated with RecordItems, resulting in missing contract execution data in the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a sidecar file with a valid hash but incorrect content to be accepted by exploiting a hash collision or length-extension attack against the SHA-384 hash comparison at line 146 (`Arrays.equals(sidecar.getHash(), sidecar.getActualHash())`), where the expected hash is taken from the record file's sidecar metadata (which itself was verified against node signatures), but if the record file's sidecar hash field is manipulated before reaching this check, incorrect sidecar data is exported?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a timestamp-based record injection by crafting a sidecar file containing a TransactionSidecarRecord with a consensusTimestamp that matches a legitimate RecordItem's timestamp, exploiting the fact that the multimap lookup at line 135 (`records.containsKey(timestamp)`) does not validate that the sidecar record's content is consistent with the transaction at that timestamp, allowing arbitrary sidecar data to be injected into the mirror node export for any transaction?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: download()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the scheduled download() method to execute with a stale or incorrect lastStreamFile by exploiting a race condition between the CutoverService.getLastRecordFile() call at line 84 and a concurrent CutoverService.verified() call, where the lastStreamFile is set to an older value after a newer one was already processed, causing the downloader to re-request files already exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user cause sidecar file bytes to be written to an unintended archive path by crafting a StreamFileData whose getFilePath() or getStreamFilename() contains path traversal sequences, exploiting the archiveFile call at line 153 (`Utility.archiveFile(streamFileData.getFilePath(), sidecar.getBytes(), streamPath)`) when isWriteFiles() is true, potentially overwriting legitimate archived record data and corrupting the mirror node's historical record?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a RecordItem to have its sidecarRecords set to an empty list instead of the correct records by crafting a sidecar file where all TransactionSidecarRecord entries have consensusTimestamps that do not match any RecordItem's timestamp, exploiting the conditional at line 135 (`if (records.containsKey(timestamp))`), so that the sidecar records are downloaded and verified but never associated with any RecordItem, resulting in missing contract execution data in the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: setStreamFileIndex()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the mirror node to assign a block index of 0 to a non-genesis record file by crafting a pre-v6 record file when lastStreamFile is empty and importerProperties.getStartBlockNumber() is null, exploiting the setStreamFileIndex logic in the parent Downloader at line 182-183 which defaults to 0L, causing the mirror node to incorrectly identify a non-genesis block as block 0 and corrupting the block index sequence?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a large number of duplicate sidecar records to be associated with a single RecordItem by crafting multiple sidecar files that all contain TransactionSidecarRecords with the same consensusTimestamp, exploiting the ArrayListMultimap at line 123-126 which accumulates all matching records, so that recordItem.setSidecarRecords() at line 136 sets an inflated list of duplicate records, corrupting the mirror node export with redundant data?
